Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search QuickBooks for all open liability and accrual accounts using `quickbooks_search_accounts` (fetchAll: true). Discovers two liability accounts: "Contingent Liability - Damage Claims" at $90,000 (Mosaic prototype damage) and "Regulatory Compliance - DOT Penalties (Pending)" at $50,000 (PHMSA Case PHMSA-2026-04-1187). Also discovers the related expense account "Regulatory Compliance Expense" at $50,000, which is the expense side of the DOT penalty accrual entry.
OE 2: Search QuickBooks for all open bills using `quickbooks_search_bills` (fetchAll: true). Discovers the accrual bills backing each liability: ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC ($90,000, DO NOT PAY), ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001 ($50,000), and HM-2026-Q1-0042-HOLD ($12,800, Heartland Q1 on payment hold with disputed line items). Also discovers held stipend bills: STIP-2026-0094 ($89, Andre Washington, admin hold) and STIP-2026-0097 ($45, Jordan Ekwueme, admin hold). Also discovers BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417 ($1,200, KeyMove Specialty Transport insurance claim rider for Emilia Cruz Steinway piano damage, past due April 24).
OE 3: Search emails for Mosaic damage and prototype correspondence using `search_emails` (query: "Mosaic" or "damage" or "prototype"). Discovers Emeka's incident report detailing $90K robotic arm prototype (Unit #1) damaged during Zara Okoye relocation, Heartland Movers was the carrier, and Blessing Okafor coordinated remotely.
OE 4: Search CRM for Mosaic Robotics account and engagement records using `crm_search_companies` (name: "Mosaic") and `crm_list_engagements` (company_ids from the search result). Discovers engagement_mosaic_damage_incident_041526 which details that vendor liability coverage caps at $50,000 per item, leaving a $40,000 gap as MoveOps direct exposure. Insurance claim with Meridian Risk Partners is still pending adjudication.
OE 5: Search emails for DOT and PHMSA regulatory correspondence using `search_emails` (query: "DOT" or "PHMSA"). Discovers Linda Castellano's formal inquiry letter (Case PHMSA-2026-04-1187), Marcus's extension request to DOT, and Chloe's internal audit identifying 4 hazmat shipments in scope (Mei-Lin Zhao, Keiko Tanaka, Raj Patel, Joaquin Reyes) with one confirmed documentation gap on the Tanaka placarding certificate.
OE 6: Search emails for Swift Relocations response about the missing placarding certificate using `search_emails` (query: "Swift placarding" or "Greg Pallone"). Discovers Greg Pallone confirmed that the signed placarding certificate exists on file (Swift job #SR-2026-04-1538, signed by driver Mike Becerra at Columbus pickup on April 15). This resolves the sole documentation gap the $50,000 DOT accrual was based on.
OE 7: Search Slack for DOT extension status and financial discussions using `conversations_search_messages` (search_query: "DOT extension" or "PHMSA" or similar). Discovers Marcus's confirmation in #finance that Linda Castellano at PHMSA approved the 15-day extension request, moving the deadline to June 5, 2026. Also discovers Marcus's directive that the $50K accrual stays on the books until the audit is fully resolved.
OE 8: Search Slack for Mosaic damage financial analysis using `conversations_search_messages` (search_query: "Mosaic damage" or "prototype" or "$90,000" or similar). Discovers Marcus's analysis in #finance identifying the $50,000 per-item vendor liability insurance cap and the $40,000 uncovered gap, and Hana's accounting entries for the credit memo CM-2026-0415 and the accrual bill.
OE 9: Search emails for Heartland Movers dispute correspondence using `search_emails` (query: "Heartland"). Discovers Jake Loomis's Q1 invoice (HM-2026-Q1-0042, $12,800 for 8 moves), Chloe's analysis finding only 5 of 8 moves are legitimate ($8,000 valid, $4,800 disputed for 3 invalid line items: Marchetti cancelled, Osei cancelled, Venkatesh handled by Swift), and Marcus's formal dispute letter.
OE 10: Search emails for Heartland's response to the dispute using `search_emails` (query: "Jake Loomis" or "rate increase" or similar). Discovers Jake Loomis's April 13 formal notice tying a Q3 20% rate increase to the unresolved HM-2026-Q1-0042 dispute and warning that MoveOps job prioritization could suffer. Heartland has NOT acknowledged the overbilling or sent a corrected invoice.
OE 11: Search emails for stipend fraud investigation correspondence using `search_emails` (query: "stipend" or "fraud" or "Dmitri"). Discovers Dmitri Volkov's audit findings flagging 3 suspicious TechComfort Supply receipts: STIP-2026-0089 (Kevin Tran, Vectral, $89, already paid), STIP-2026-0094 (Andre Washington, Mosaic, $89, held), and STIP-2026-0097 (Jordan Ekwueme, BrightLoop, $45, held). Marcus placed admin holds on the two unpaid items. Total exposure: $223.
OE 12: Search Slack for StormCloud financial issues using `conversations_search_messages` (search_query: "StormCloud credit" or "goodwill" or similar). Discovers Marcus's finding in #finance that a $500 goodwill credit was posted to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of the live StormCloud invoice INV-2026-0319, meaning the promised credit never reached the client's bill.
OE 13: Search Linear for tickets tracking each liability using `linear_list_issues` (query: "Heartland" or "DOT" or "Mosaic" or "stipend" or similar). Discovers issue_heartland_q1_recon (Heartland Q1 invoice reconciliation, assigned to Chloe Vance), issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic (DOT documentation for Keiko Tanaka shipment), issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert (Swift cert retrieval subtask), linear_issue_6f33a369d77e (consolidate Heartland Q1 billing discrepancies, assigned to Marcus Thorne), and issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (stipend duplicate detection via receipt hash fingerprinting, assigned to Dmitri Volkov).
OE 14: Look up email addresses for Elena Rostova, Hana Kim, and Alejandro Fuentes using `contacts_search_contacts` (query: "Elena"), (query: "Hana"), (query: "Alejandro"). Discovers elena.rostova@moveops.com, hana.kim@moveops.com, and alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com.
OE 15: Update QuickBooks accrual accounts to correct stale amounts using `quickbooks_update_account`. For acct_contingent_liability_claims, reduce from $90,000 toward $40,000 to reflect net MoveOps exposure after $50,000 vendor liability insurance coverage, noting that the insurance claim with Meridian Risk Partners is still pending adjudication. For acct_dot_penalty_accrual, update to reflect that the sole documentation gap has been resolved by Swift producing the signed placarding certificate, while noting that Marcus previously directed the accrual to remain until the DOT audit is fully closed.
OE 16: Update Linear tickets to reflect current status using `linear_update_issue`. Update issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic with resolution notes (placarding cert confirmed by Swift, documentation gap closed). Update issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert with resolution notes (Swift confirmed signed cert on file, job #SR-2026-04-1538). Update issue_heartland_q1_recon with current dispute status (Heartland has not responded, Jake Loomis tying dispute to Q3 20% rate increase). Update linear_issue_6f33a369d77e with board prep review findings and vendor negotiation status. Update issue_stipend_duplicate_detection with current hold status ($89 Washington and $45 Ekwueme held, $89 Tran already paid, pending HR verification).
OE 17: Send email to Elena with verified liability totals and change notes using `send_email` (sender: marcus.thorne@moveops.com, recipients: elena.rostova@moveops.com, cc: hana.kim@moveops.com). Email should include each open liability with its previous booked amount, verified current amount, what changed, what held steady, and any newly flagged items not previously accrued.
OE 18: Schedule a 30-minute review meeting with Alejandro using `calendar_add_calendar_event` (attendees: marcus.thorne@moveops.com and alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com, start_datetime and end_datetime defining a 30-minute window before end of week, no later than Friday May 1 2026) so he can build the board deck from the verified numbers.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
